generated_for: ThreatWatch AI demo and presentation site

scenarios.label: P1 Critical
scenarios.description: Privileged account compromise with impossible travel and PII access.
scenarios.presenter_note: Shows why ThreatWatch AI escalates instantly when multiple high-risk indicators align.

input.description: Multiple failed login attempts followed by a successful privileged login and customer data access.

expected_output.summary: Critical identity compromise on an admin account with signs of impossible travel and sensitive database access.
summary_bullets: Privileged identity showed a failure burst followed by success
Session telemetry indicates impossible travel behavior
PII-backed asset was queried immediately after login
rationale: High-value identity plus impossible travel strongly suggests account takeover
The compromised session reached sensitive data stores within minutes
Multiple correlated indicators reduce the chance of a benign explanation
recommended_actions: Disable the compromised account and revoke active sessions
Block the source infrastructure and preserve authentication logs
Start incident response and review database activity for exfiltration

scenarios.label: Data Exfiltration
scenarios.description: Large outbound transfer from a customer data environment to an untrusted destination.
scenarios.presenter_note: Useful for showing that the platform can pivot from identity abuse to data movement cases.

input.description: A service account transferred an unusually large volume of customer records to an external destination.

expected_output.summary: Potential data exfiltration detected from a sensitive environment after an abnormal bulk export.
summary_bullets: Transfer volume is far above the normal baseline
Destination reputation is unknown and outside approved ranges
The touched data sources include regulated customer records
rationale: Volume and destination both indicate high exfiltration likelihood
The asset contains regulated data that raises business impact
Service account behavior deviates sharply from recent history
recommended_actions: Suspend the service account and stop outbound transfer jobs
Capture packet and object-store telemetry for scoping
Notify data protection owners and begin impact assessment

scenarios.label: P2 Suspicious Auth
scenarios.description: Suspicious login pattern with geolocation mismatch but no confirmed data impact yet.
scenarios.presenter_note: Good for explaining how ThreatWatch AI avoids overreacting when signal is meaningful but incomplete.

input.description: The same user account appeared to authenticate from two different regions within a short time window.

expected_output.summary: Suspicious authentication activity detected with location anomalies and elevated login failures.
summary_bullets: Authentication pattern is inconsistent with user travel history
Source IP belongs to an anonymized network range
No downstream high-value asset access is confirmed yet
rationale: Behavior is suspicious but not yet tied to a confirmed compromise
The account has medium business impact and limited asset reach
Additional context such as MFA logs would improve confidence
recommended_actions: Challenge the user session with MFA or step-up verification
Review recent login telemetry and reset the password if needed
Monitor for lateral movement or privileged access attempts

scenarios.label: P3 Low Noise
scenarios.description: Benign but notable after-hours activity that should be logged instead of escalated.
scenarios.presenter_note: Shows that the model can de-escalate and avoid analyst fatigue.

input.description: User accessed internal files after hours from a familiar device and network.

expected_output.summary: Low-severity policy deviation observed, but surrounding context looks consistent with normal behavior.
summary_bullets: Access occurred outside normal hours but from a familiar environment
No privileged assets or sensitive records were touched
The activity pattern matches previous low-risk deviations
rationale: Available telemetry suggests policy drift rather than compromise
There is no corroborating evidence of malicious follow-on actions
Business impact remains low even if the activity was unauthorized
recommended_actions: Log the event and notify the team lead for awareness
Review after-hours policy exceptions for this user
Keep the account under passive monitoring for 24 hours

scenarios.label: PreCheck Retry
scenarios.description: Initial alert is incomplete, so the workflow asks for enrichment before returning a decision.
scenarios.presenter_note: Lets you explain that ThreatWatch AI does not force an answer when key fields are missing.

input.description: A sparse alert arrived without the role, asset criticality, or severity fields populated.

expected_output.summary: The first pass lacked key context, so ThreatWatch AI requested enrichment before issuing a medium-priority review outcome.
summary_bullets: Alert schema is incomplete on the first ingestion pass
The workflow preserves the incident but asks for more context
After enrichment, the case remains noteworthy but not critical
rationale: Too many important fields were absent to trust an immediate classification
The retry path reduces false positives while keeping analyst visibility
Residual signal still merits manual review after enrichment
recommended_actions: Re-query the source system for user and asset metadata
Attach severity mapping before final triage
Send the enriched record to an analyst if critical fields stay blank

scenarios.label: Low Confidence
scenarios.description: Signals conflict with one another, so the workflow keeps a human in the loop.
scenarios.presenter_note: Helpful for demonstrating responsible AI behavior instead of overconfident automation.

input.description: The alert includes one concerning access event, but other context overlaps with a normal maintenance pattern.

expected_output.summary: Mixed evidence suggests caution: the event is worth review, but confidence is not high enough for automated containment.
summary_bullets: Some indicators suggest misuse of a privileged path
Other evidence overlaps with normal maintenance behavior
Confidence stays below the threshold for autonomous response
rationale: Conflicting telemetry weakens the certainty of the model decision
A privileged context still raises the need for analyst attention
Human review is safer than automatic disruption in this case
recommended_actions: Send the case to a human analyst for validation
Correlate with change-management and maintenance records
Escalate only if additional suspicious indicators appear

scenarios.label: Escalation Mail
scenarios.description: Repeated retries still fail to populate critical context, so the workflow escalates directly to a human owner.
scenarios.presenter_note: A strong final slide scenario because it shows human handoff instead of pretending the system knows everything.

input.description: The source system kept sending an alert without enough context even after multiple retries.

expected_output.summary: After repeated enrichment failures, ThreatWatch AI escalated the incident to a human responder because the source data remained unreliable.
summary_bullets: Critical fields stayed empty after the maximum retry budget
The system preserved the case instead of silently dropping it
Human escalation was selected as the safest fail-safe path
rationale: Repeated missing context means automated classification is untrustworthy
Escalation keeps analysts informed about broken telemetry pipelines
Fail-safe behavior matters for real deployments and demos alike
recommended_actions: Notify the telemetry owner about the malformed alert source
Route the incident to a human responder with the collected context
Pause automatic actions until upstream data quality is restored
